generated_oml:
// C1: Digital Shadow Components
instance ManufacturingContextDigitalShadow : DTDFVocab:DigitalShadow [
    base:desc "Centralized aggregation of contextual data traces from distributed manufacturing sensors for passive monitoring and abstraction purposes"
]

instance OperationalParameterDigitalShadow : DTDFVocab:DigitalShadow [
    base:desc "Specialized digital shadow focused on operational metrics (e.g., temperature, pressure, motion) from IoT-enabled devices in manufacturing environments"
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionDigitalShadow : DTDFVocab:DigitalShadow [
    base:desc "Aggregates environmental metrics (e.g., humidity, temperature profiles) for contextual data traces in manufacturing systems"
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance StateMachineBehaviorEngine : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Active component implementing state machines/automata for behavioral modeling of Digital Twin system behaviors (e.g., MontiArc/SysML v1.7)"
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Simulation tool (e.g., MathWorks Simulink) enabling predictive analytics and closed-loop testing via continuous/discrete-event modeling"
]

instance RuleBasedDecisionProcessor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Software interpreter processing digital shadow data for rule-based or probabilistic decision-making (e.g., state transition rules)"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled temperature sensor for real-time monitoring of manufacturing process conditions"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTrace
]

instance PressureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled pressure sensor for operational parameter monitoring in manufacturing environments"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PressureDataTrace
]

instance MotionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled motion sensor for process state tracking in distributed manufacturing systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotionDataTrace
]

instance EnvironmentalParameterSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental monitoring device collecting contextual data traces (humidity, temperature profiles) for manufacturing processes"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTrace
]

// C4: Data Transmission
instance TemperatureDataTrace : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance PressureDataTrace : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PressureSensor
]

instance MotionDataTrace : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotionSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalDataTrace : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalParameterSensor
]

// C5: Data Aggregation Components
instance ContextualDataAggregator : DTDFVocab:DataAggregator [
    base:desc "Software component aggregating raw sensor data into contextual data traces with abstraction and normalization for Digital Shadow purposes"
]

instance ManufacturingDataProcessor : DTDFVocab:DataAggregator [
    base:desc "Specialized processor handling manufacturing-specific data aggregation and feature extraction for Digital Twin integration"
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Active service providing real-time monitoring of manufacturing processes through aggregated Digital Shadow data and behavioral models"
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessStateInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling predictive modeling using state machines and simulation tools to forecast system states or failures"
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Active component processing digital shadow data to derive actionable optimization insights for manufacturing processes"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationAction
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Active Digital Twin component enabling autonomous decision-making and closed-loop feedback to physical manufacturing systems"
    DTDFVocab:provides AutonomousControlAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration with simulation tools for interactive visualization of system states across abstraction layers (SysML, Simulink, C++)"
    DTDFVocab:provides StateVisualizationInsight
]

// C7: Insights/Actions
instance ProcessStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Contextualized state traces aggregated from Digital Shadows providing passive monitoring context for real-time monitoring service"
]

instance FailurePredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive modeling results derived from behavioral models (state machines) and simulation outputs for failure forecasting"
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Actionable recommendations generated from rule-based processing of digital shadow data for process optimization"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AutonomousControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Autonomous decision-making actions executed by Digital Twin to adjust manufacturing processes based on real-time analytics"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance StateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization outputs from simulation tools showing system states, behaviors, and performance metrics across abstraction layers"
]

// C8: Models
instance ManufacturingBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model using state machines (SysML v1.7) to represent dynamic manufacturing process behaviors"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateMachineBehaviorEngine
]

instance PhysicsBasedSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation model implemented in MathWorks Simulink for predictive analytics and closed-loop testing"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine
]

instance DigitalShadowAbstractionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model defining abstraction rules for contextual data traces collected by Digital Shadows in manufacturing environments"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContextualDataAggregator
]

// C9: Data
instance RawTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Unprocessed temperature readings from IoT sensors in manufacturing processes"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContextualDataAggregator
]

instance ProcessContextData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces combining temperature, pressure, and motion metrics for process state monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTrace, PressureDataTrace, MotionDataTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateMachineBehaviorEngine, SimulationEngine
]

instance EnvironmentalContextData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated environmental metrics (humidity, temperature profiles) providing contextual background for manufacturing processes"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalDataTrace
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContextualDataAggregator
]

// C10: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modeling language (SysML) for component-level modeling including state machines and interaction block diagrams"
    DTDFVocab:enables ManufacturingBehaviorModel, ManufacturingContextDigitalShadow
]

instance MontiArcAutomaton : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton framework for behavioral modeling and state machine implementations in Digital Twin systems"
    DTDFVocab:enables StateMachineBehaviorEngine
]

instance SimulinkSimulationPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink platform for physics-based simulations and predictive analytics in Digital Twin implementations"
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicsBasedSimulationModel, SimulationEngine
]

instance CPlusPlusImplementation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "General-purpose C++ platform for implementing DT logic, control algorithms, and data processing pipelines"
    DTDFVocab:enables RuleBasedDecisionProcessor, ControlService
]

instance DigitalShadowFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework defining digital shadow architecture for contextual data traces and aggregation in manufacturing environments"
    DTDFVocab:enables OperationalParameterDigitalShadow, EnvironmentalConditionDigitalShadow
]

// C11: Environment
instance ManufacturingProcessEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains TemperatureSensor, PressureSensor, MotionSensor,
                 EnvironmentalParameterSensor, ManufacturingContextDigitalShadow,
                 ManufacturingBehaviorModel, PhysicsBasedSimulationModel
]

// C12: Component Relationships
instance ManufacturingSystemComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Core manufacturing system components including sensors, processing units, and physical assets being modeled in Digital Twin"
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingProcessEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "A Digital Twin system in this context does not explicitly define synchronization frequencies, latency requirements, or temporal granularity needs. However, the distinction between **Digital Shadows** and **Digital Twins** implies that while a **Digital Shadow** operates as a **passive, one-way data flow** (e.g., automated aggregation of contextual data traces with abstraction for specific purposes), a **Digital Twin** would require **active components** to interpret and process incoming data in real-time or near-real-time. The temporal scale for DTs is implied to involve **dynamic synchronization** between physical and digital components, though no specific frequencies (e.g., millisecond, second-level updates) or granularity (e.g., event-based vs. time-triggered) are detailed. The documents emphasize that **Digital Shadows** lack bidirectional communication, whereas **Digital Twins** enable **active data processing**, suggesting a higher temporal resolution requirement for DTs than Shadows."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** is not explicitly defined in the documents, but the distinction between **Digital Shadows** (passive, one-way data flow) and **Digital Twins** (active, bidirectional processing) implies a **system-wide coordination framework** for orchestration. While the documents do not specify architecture patterns (e.g., microservices, modular design), the **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., SysML, ADLs like AADL, MontiArc) suggest that a Digital Twin system would integrate **component-level models** (e.g., components, connectors, state machines) with **behavioral simulations** (e.g., Simulink, C++/Java code). The **resource management** would likely involve **heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives** (e.g., engineering, operational) and **multi-tool integration** (e.g., SysML, simulation tools, code), ensuring interoperability across abstraction layers (requirements → functional → logical → technical). The absence of explicit constellation details implies a **flexible, multi-layered architecture** underpinned by MBSE principles."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  A Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the described context spans structured modeling phases aligned with Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) methodologies. The lifecycle is segmented into **four primary abstraction levels**:
  1. **Requirements Phase**: Defines functional and non-functional specifications using **SysML** or other ADLs (e.g., AADL, ACME) to capture system requirements.
  2. **Functional/Logical Architecture Phase**: Models components and connectors via **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **MontiArc** or **SysML IBDs**, emphasizing component interfaces, interconnections, and composition. Behavior is detailed using **MontiArc Automata** or **state machines** in SysML v1.7.
  3. **Product/Technical Layer**: Integrates simulation tools (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink**, other simulation frameworks) or code (e.g., **C++/Java**) to validate behavior and performance.
  **Transition Management**: The lifecycle transitions are governed by **multi-perspective integration** (e.g., heterogeneous stakeholders’ views) and **methodologies such as RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics), OOSEM, SPES, or SMArDT**, ensuring consistency across abstraction layers. **Lifecycle governance** is implicitly implied by the structured ADL-based modeling workflows, though explicit governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC 15288) are not referenced.

  **Note**: The document does not specify explicit lifecycle stages (e.g., 'Design,' 'Deployment,' 'Operation') or transition triggers (e.g., validation thresholds), but the described **multi-layered abstraction** implies a phased evolution from conceptual requirements to executable simulations.
 "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
  **Model fidelity** is ensured through:
  - **Validation/verification methods**: Implicitly tied to **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) for aligning models with system requirements and functional architectures ([18]–[22]).
  - **Uncertainty quantification**: Not explicitly addressed, but implied to be managed via **software interpretation** of aggregated data traces ([32]).
  - **Error detection/correction**: Not detailed, but **active components** in the DT (vs. passive digital shadows) may include **state machines** or **automata** (e.g., MontiArc) for behavioral validation ([25]).
  - **Quality assurance**: Enforced through **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **toolchain interoperability** (e.g., SysML, ADLs, or simulation tools) to ensure consistency across abstraction layers ([31]).

  **Digital shadows** lack autonomous feedback, so fidelity relies on **contextual aggregation** and **software processing** of raw data traces ([32]).
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Network Infrastructure for Digital Shadows**:
  The provided context does not explicitly describe a **Digital Twin (DT)** but focuses on **digital shadows**, which are **passive** and unidirectional. However, the **communication flow** between physical and digital entities is implied to rely on:
  - **Communication Protocols**: Likely **industrial IoT protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) for bidirectional data exchange (though not detailed for DTs).
  - **Network Topology**: Not specified, but the **manufacturing context** (Fig. 1) suggests a **hierarchical topology** (e.g., edge-cloud architecture) with **redundant paths** for reliability.
  - **Bandwidth/Latency Requirements**: Not quantified, but the term 'aggregation' implies **low-latency** requirements for real-time data processing, possibly constrained by **edge computing** for latency-sensitive applications.
  - **Reliability/Redundancy**: Not detailed, but the **passive nature** of digital shadows suggests **failover mechanisms** (e.g., dual communication links) to ensure uninterrupted data flow.
  - **Security Protocols**: Not mentioned, but the **manufacturing context** (Fig. 1) implies adherence to **industry standards** (e.g., TLS for data encryption, access control via **IEC 62443**).
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  **Deployment Infrastructure for Digital Shadows**:
  The provided context does not explicitly describe **Digital Twin hosting/deployment**, but the **modeling methodologies** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) and **MBSE practices** imply the following for a DT system:
  - **Hosting Platforms**: Not specified, but the **manufacturing domain** suggests **cloud-edge hybrid** deployment (e.g., AWS IoT Greengrass for edge processing, AWS EC2 for analytics).
  - **Computational Requirements**: Not quantified, but the **multi-layered modeling** (requirements → logical architecture → behavior) implies **high-performance computing** (e.g., GPUs for simulation, high-memory nodes for large-scale data aggregation).
  - **Storage Requirements**: Not detailed, but the **contextual data traces** imply **long-term storage** (e.g., **time-series databases** like InfluxDB or **distributed storage** like S3) for historical analysis.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**: Not specified, but the **heterogeneous stakeholders** (Sec. II.A) suggest **modular architectures** (e.g., microservices for DT components) to handle dynamic workloads.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: Not detailed, but the **passive digital shadow** model implies **SLA-based redundancy** (e.g., 99.9% uptime via failover clusters) and **automated failover** mechanisms.
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  The described Digital Twin (DT) system adheres to **industry-specific and engineering standards** relevant to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** and **Digital Shadow integration**, though no explicit certification frameworks are detailed. Key standards include:

  **Industry/Engineering Standards**:
  - **SysML** (System Modeling Language) for requirements and architecture modeling.
  - **AADL (Aircraft Adapters and Languages)** or **ACME** for component-based system description.
  - **MontiArc** (for state-based modeling) and **SysML v1.7** (for behavioral extensions).
  - **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Kritzinger et al., 2023) defines **communication protocols** (one-way data flow) and **data aggregation abstractions**, though no specific protocols (e.g., MQTT, REST) are named.

  **Compliance Frameworks**:
  - **ISO/IEC 15288** (Systems Engineering Life Cycle Processes) is implied by the **structured lifecycle phases** (requirements → architecture → simulation), though not explicitly referenced.
  - **Interoperability standards** are implied by **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** (e.g., ADLs + simulation tools + code), but no specific standards (e.g., OPC UA, ROS) are mentioned.

  **Certification Requirements**:
  - Not in Document: No mention of **third-party certifications** (e.g., ISO 27001 for security, IEC 62443 for industrial control systems).

  **Note**: The document emphasizes **methodological compliance** (e.g., RFLP, ADLs) over **technical standards**, with Digital Shadows and DTs operating under **implicit interoperability assumptions**.
  "
]
